# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request Build Notification 
on: [pull_request]   # Triggered on every pull request to the repository   
jobs:                 
  build-notifications:      # job name is unique per workflow execution. This will be used as an identifier for this particular running instance of your worklow in a specific runner's session, e.g., macos or linux/windows based on where you run it from Terminal (git bash etc)  
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest  # define the type and version of container to be used by these jobs that this job invokes    
    steps:                  
      - name: Checkout Repository  # This action will check out your code. Useful when you want a clean copy from repository  
        uses: actions/checkout@v2      
        
      - name: Notify with GitHub Action (Pull Request Title and Description)   
        id: notify                   # A unique identifier for the step, optional but recommended    
        env:                          # set environment variables using secrets or a token.  Useful when you want to pass sensitive data such as passwords  
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN}}    # Token used for authenticate the action with GitHub    
        run: |                         # Run steps in a shell before running your build command  e,g to set environment variable on macOS or Linux   Useful when you want do some operations such as `chmod` and others.      Actions/github-script@v3 is another way of doing this which support more complex scripting features
          echo "::set-env name=BRANCH_NAME name=$GITHUB_HEAD_REF"       # Store result in the environment variable BRANCH_NAME  This can be used later as ${{ env.BRANCH_NAME }}   You may use this to store your results using GitHub's Secret feature
          echo "Pull Request Title:   ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" # Pulls pull request title from event and stores in GITHUB_HEAD variable as a comment  Can be used later for logging   You may use this to log the PR title using GitHub's Comments feature
          echo "Pull Request Description: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}" # Pulls pull request description from event and stores in GITHUB_HEAD variable as a comment  Can be used later for logging   You may use this to log the PR body using GitHub's Comments feature
          echo "Commit Message: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }} has been merged into branch  ${{ env.BRANCH_NAME }}" # Sets commit message with Pull Request Title and Branch name, can be used to create release notes or similar in the PR body  
          echo "::add-mask::$GITHUB_TOKEN"  # Masking Token for output purpose only. Not exposed when using ::set-env action    echos are not required unless you want a specific command line step to be executed after all environment variable setup steps due to their verbosity
